description: Expanded RAGAS eval set: 10 questions per policy level (1–6), anchored to legal-boundary chunk IDs.

samples.user_input: Before the Public Service Commission makes an appointment, what must it do regarding the relevant Minister?
samples.reference: The Commission must consult with the relevant Minister and take into consideration the Minister's views and requirements before making an appointment.

samples.user_input: What recruitment and appointment matters are among the principal functions of the Public Service Commission?
samples.reference: The Commission determines practices and procedures relating to recruitment and appointment of employees, promotion, employment, transfer, secondment, redeployment, discipline, and termination.

samples.user_input: How does the Public Service Act define a Ministry?
samples.reference: A Ministry means any ministry, department or office listed in Schedule I of the Act.

samples.user_input: Does each Ministry have a Chief Executive Officer under the Public Service Act?
samples.reference: Yes. There shall be a Chief Executive Officer or Chief Executive Officers for each Ministry.

samples.user_input: What does Section 4B of the Public Service Act say about managing the Public Service?
samples.reference: Section 4B includes providing a legal framework for the effective and efficient management and leadership of the Public Service.

samples.user_input: What happens if a public servant breaches the Code of Ethics and Conduct?
samples.reference: A breach of the Code is deemed a breach of discipline under the Public Service (Disciplinary Procedures) Regulations as amended.

samples.user_input: Can a public servant use their official status to obtain a personal advantage under the Code of Ethics?
samples.reference: No. They must not use their duty, status, power or authority to gain or seek to gain a benefit or advantage for themselves or any other person.

samples.user_input: What is the short title of the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct?
samples.reference: It may be cited as the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct 2010.

samples.user_input: When may a public servant make a public statement or engage in political activity under the Code?
samples.reference: They must refrain from making public statements or engaging in political activity contrary to the Amendment Act 2010 without prior approval of the Chief Executive Officer.

samples.user_input: What does the Code require regarding accountability in relationships with the public?
samples.reference: Public servants must be accountable for their actions.

samples.user_input: Who may impose sanctions for a minor breach of discipline under the Disciplinary Procedures Regulations?
samples.reference: The Chief Executive Officer may impose sanctions after finding that an employee committed a minor breach of discipline.

samples.user_input: What must a Chief Executive Officer do when there is a serious financial irregularity?
samples.reference: The Chief Executive Officer shall initiate disciplinary action by giving a complete report to the Chief Executive Officer of the Commission.

samples.user_input: How must documents be served for disciplinary proceedings before the Charge Formulation Committee, Commission, or Tribunal?
samples.reference: Personal service of any document is required for proceedings before the Charge Formulation Committee, the Commission, or the Tribunal.

samples.user_input: Who is the employer for grievance resolution under the Grievance and Dispute Procedures Regulations?
samples.reference: For Part II of those Regulations, employer means the Chief Executive Officer or the Commission.

samples.user_input: How long does an employee have to appeal a grievance decision?
samples.reference: The employee must file an appeal with the Secretary within 14 days of receipt of the decision, together with the prescribed fee in Schedule II.

samples.user_input: What must a Head of Division do when notified of an alleged fraud?
samples.reference: The Head of Division must take action in accordance with the Fraud Policy.

samples.user_input: When must asset management perform a physical stock-take under the Fraud Policy?
samples.reference: Asset management must perform a physical asset count at the end of each financial year to verify assets on the register, as required under Section 5 of the Treasury Instructions 2020.

samples.user_input: What must Chief Executive Officers ensure about recording attendance and overtime under Policy 39?
samples.reference: They must issue detailed internal instructions to accounting officers, time-keepers and supervisors on how attendance and authorised overtime are recorded and checked.

samples.user_input: What is the premise of the Public Service Sexual Harassment Policy?
samples.reference: The policy reflects and reinforces principles and practices that apply to conduct in all Government workplaces.

samples.user_input: How does the Fraud Policy define a bribe?
samples.reference: A bribe means the giving, offering or acceptance of any money or valuable consideration by any person, whether within or outside Government, to any person.

samples.user_input: How much special leave may employees selected for overseas national sporting tours receive?
samples.reference: They may be granted special leave of absence for up to 40 working days per annum.

samples.user_input: Who is the Chief Executive Officer of the Commission under the Policy Instructions?
samples.reference: The Chief Executive Officer of the Office of the Public Service Commission appointed under the Public Service Act.

samples.user_input: When do salary rewards and band movements take effect under Instruction 5A.7?
samples.reference: Salary grants and movement up the band are linked to performance at post and are effective from 1 July of every financial year.

samples.user_input: How is a Chief Executive Officer performance agreement prepared under Instruction 3A.1.1?
samples.reference: The Chief Executive Officer's performance agreement is prepared annually by the Chief Executive Officer and agreed with the Minister.

samples.user_input: Can an employee compulsorily retired on ill-health grounds be re-employed without a medical report?
samples.reference: No. They shall not be re-employed without a medical report from the Chief Executive Officer for the Ministry of Health.

samples.user_input: Can public servants post illegal or pornographic content on personal social media?
samples.reference: No. Employees must not use personal social media to post information that breaches any law of Tonga, including pornographic material, child pornography, or child abuse content.

samples.user_input: When may employees use personal social media accounts for official ministry work?
samples.reference: Exceptions apply for social media administrators who must log onto personal accounts to manage ministry platforms, and for employees informing the public about initiatives, projects, events, or important work.

samples.user_input: Who should be the first responder for political neutrality concerns under the guidelines?
samples.reference: Ministry Human Resources units should be the first responders and must be well versed in the Code and applicable policies.

samples.user_input: Which legislation do the Political Neutrality Guidelines cite as their basis?
samples.reference: They cite Section 4B of the Public Service Act, which sets out objectives of the Act.

samples.user_input: What freedom-of-expression principle appears in Section 4 of the Social Media Guideline?
samples.reference: Section 4 states that it shall be lawful for people to speak, write and print their opinions, with freedom of speech and of the press.

samples.user_input: Is there a Public Service Commission established under the Public Service Act?
samples.reference: Yes. There shall be established a Public Service Commission.

samples.user_input: How does the Public Service Act define the Commission?
samples.reference: The Commission means the Public Service Commission established under the Act.

samples.user_input: Who counts as an employee under the Public Service Act?
samples.reference: An employee means all persons employed in the Public Service.

samples.user_input: Who may recommend that a public service post be made redundant?
samples.reference: The Commission may, in consultation with the Chief Executive Officer, recommend to the relevant Minister that any post be made redundant.

samples.user_input: What does the Public Service Act say about the political neutrality of the Public Service?
samples.reference: The Public Service is apolitical, performing its functions in an impartial, professional and competent manner.

samples.user_input: What does the Code of Ethics require about fulfilling obligations to Government?
samples.reference: Public servants must fulfil their lawful obligations to the Government with professionalism and integrity.

samples.user_input: Must public servants comply with applicable policies and procedures under the Code of Ethics?
samples.reference: Yes. They must comply with all applicable laws, regulations, policies, instructions and procedures.

samples.user_input: What standard of discretion does the Code of Ethics require of public servants?
samples.reference: They must act with appropriate discretion.

samples.user_input: What must public servants avoid to protect the reputation of their Ministry under the Code?
samples.reference: They must avoid any activity, whether connected with official duties or otherwise, that might bring their Ministry and the Public Service into disrepute.

samples.user_input: What is one purpose of the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct?
samples.reference: It provides guidance to employees on the standards of behaviour required of them.

samples.user_input: Who decides whether a breach of discipline is minor or serious under the Disciplinary Procedures Regulations?
samples.reference: Subject to the Regulations, a Chief Executive Officer or the Commission shall decide whether a breach of discipline is minor or serious.

samples.user_input: How are reports of serious breach of discipline or repeated minor breaches handled?
samples.reference: A report of serious breach of discipline or repeated minor breaches of discipline shall be dealt with as prescribed in Regulation 7.

samples.user_input: Must an employment grievance be kept confidential under the Grievance Regulations?
samples.reference: Yes. The employment grievance shall be kept confidential between the parties.

samples.user_input: How quickly must an employer meet with an employee after receiving a grievance notice?
samples.reference: The employer shall call a meeting with the employee within 5 working days from receipt of the notice.

samples.user_input: How long does the Tribunal have to determine a grievance appeal?
samples.reference: The appeal shall be determined within 20 working days from the date the appeal was filed with the Secretary of the Tribunal.

samples.user_input: What is the purpose of the Public Service Policy 2010?
samples.reference: The purpose of the Policy is to set out the general policies for the Public Service.

samples.user_input: How are permanent employees appointed under Public Service Policy 2010?
samples.reference: Permanent employees shall be appointed to permanent posts funded from the annual estimates of the relevant Ministry.

samples.user_input: Who is responsible for preventing fraud in ministries under the Fraud Policy?
samples.reference: The Chief Executive Officer has the overall responsibility and accountability for the identification and prevention of fraud in the MDAs.

samples.user_input: What happens if an employee fails to comply with the Fraud Policy?
samples.reference: The employee shall be subject to disciplinary action in accordance with the Fraud Policy and the Regulations.

samples.user_input: Where should a formal sexual harassment complaint be submitted under the Sexual Harassment Policy?
samples.reference: The complaint should be submitted to the Commission through the Chief Executive Officer of the Ministry concerned; if the alleged harasser is the CEO, the complaint may be made directly to the Commission.

samples.user_input: Who is the appointing authority under the Policy Instructions?
samples.reference: The appointing authority means the agency or person with the legal authority to appoint employees.

samples.user_input: Can a permanent employee be terminated without Commission approval?
samples.reference: No. No permanent or contract employee's service may be terminated without the Commission's approval.

samples.user_input: How do the Policy Instructions define a casual employee?
samples.reference: A casual employee is a person employed on a casual basis for temporary, seasonal, or special work that does not warrant salaried employment, for a specified period not exceeding 12 months accumulated in any financial year.

samples.user_input: What are the standard working hours for public service employees under Instruction 2A.4?
samples.reference: Employees work a minimum of 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday, normally from 8:30am to 4:30pm with a one-hour meal break.

samples.user_input: What is one objective of Instruction 1A.1 on recruitment?
samples.reference: It provides guidance to the public service for implementing recruitment provisions in the Public Service Policy 2010 and relevant Cabinet decisions on public service employment.

samples.user_input: What accountability principle applies to the Public Service in Section 4 of the Social Media Guideline?
samples.reference: The Public Service shall be ethical and accountable for its actions to the public in providing satisfactory service.

samples.user_input: What is the purpose of the Political Neutrality Guidelines?
samples.reference: The guidelines assist Chief Executive Officers and ministries during election periods, drawn from the Public Service Act, Regulations, and Code of Ethics.

samples.user_input: Should normal government business continue during an election period under the Political Neutrality Guidelines?
samples.reference: Yes. Normal business of government should continue, but ministries should avoid partisanship and ensure impartiality of the public service.

samples.user_input: Can employees access personal social media on government computers during working hours?
samples.reference: No. Employees must not use a government computer or government-issued device to access personal social media during official working hours.

samples.user_input: Can civil servants engage in public political campaigning while on duty under the Political Neutrality Guidelines?
samples.reference: No. They must not engage in public campaigning, political endorsements, or wear campaign material while on duty or in an official capacity.
